original: Residential Deck Nastizia
Tavros Station
You [pc.move] up to the security buzzer to the side of the gate, press it, announce that [pc.name] Steele has arrived as requested by invitation.

The response on the other side is silence, followed by a squawk of feedback. A minute later, a familiar brown-skinned human in a security uniform opens the front gate, jerking her head for you to come inside.
“Hands out to the side,” Riya says, before roughly patting you down. “Weapons in the gatehouse. You’ll get them back when you leave.”

She gives you a slap on the ass once you’re done, which you take largely as encouragement to make haste to the front door. It’s flanked by an older, rod-backed male kaithrit and a lusciously pear-shaped purple rahn in a maid costume who smiles at you demurely as you enter.
“Darling Steele! I’m sooooo glad you could come.”

Nastizia is lounging upon a black settee in the spacious living room you are ushered, a glass of wine on the table of floating marble in front of her, a feline in a sunbeam. In substance the rooms of this place have the sterile, anodyne good taste of any expensive hotel. However, royal effort has gone into personalizing them. Above an honest-to-goodness open fireplace an oil portrait of what you assume is a high-ranking member of the Romavian family hangs; felt, gilded chairs are arranged around the floating coffee table; in front of it the scaly pelt of a green-and-yellow striped predator is lain; lush tropical plants hang from baskets and sit on ledges. Through into the dining room, impressive holographic and sound work produce a facsimile of a Tenevan villa, open to warm sunlight, a lake and tall, tree-covered hills. On the table a small, traditional kaithrit feast is laid.

“Please,” Nastizia says, indicating the spread, one fluffy tail curling around your [pc.footOrFeet]. “But not too much, darling.” Her claws graze delicately down your arm. “I want you nice and limber for the entertainments.”

Grilled, oily, eel-like fish; citrus fruit and udon-like noodle stuffed into crisp vine leaves; wine so dry and white it looks like sparkling water; plump Tenevan ishis and jerris baked in brown sugar that you dangle into your mouth by their tails; smoky Roshan tea to follow.
You so rarely get high quality kaithrit home cooking on the frontier, and you find it difficult to control yourself in front of it.
Kaithrit cooking is a little too tart and rich for your taste, but you can hardly turn it down when it’s been made to this kind of standard.
The white-furred kaithrit picks at her food and bats her eyelashes, whilst Riya leans on the door, eyes fixed on the fake middle distance. Both are eager for you to finish.
Jhoi
You [pc.move] up to the security buzzer to the side of the gate, press it, announce that once again, [pc.name] Steele has presented [pc.him]self.
“"+{var0}.mf("Monsieur","Madame")+" Steele? Mon dieu!” comes a strange, female voice. “Oh, this is all wrong. Mademoiselle must have... one moment, please!”

original: The purple rahn maid that usually holds the front door open comes hurrying up to the gate. She’s in quite the tizzy, a fact that only makes the well-stuffed cleavage in her low cut outfit jiggle all the more conspicuously.

“Please come in, "+{var0}.mf("monsieur","madame")+",” she says in her high, tinkling voice, blinking at you as she pulls the wrought plasteel aside. “Mademoiselle is out all today. She went on a, how you say, trip of the stars with her gendarme. There must have been some sort of mix up... I’m so sorry that you came all this way for nothing.” Her soft, slightly moist fingers touch your arm. The smell of horny honeysuckle fills your nose, fragrant and stirring. “I’m Jhoi. Why don’t you come in and sit down?”

original: She knows about you?

“Of course!” She giggles self-consciously, playing with her apron. “You are all over the extranet, your adventures - your father. I thought maybe you were a fantasy until Mademoiselle brought you here for first time. It is said you have rescued slaves and conquered planets. That you are a fantastic lover...”
The starchy material of her outfit creaks. Dark eyes look deep into yours.

“"+{var0}.mf("Monsieur","Madame")+" Steele,” she murmurs, “both of us, we are driven distracted by the same person. And you know she is, how you say, putting on your lead? She doesn’t mean truly to have your children I don’t think. Oh, show me what she’s missing. Right here, where she usually sits. I can tell you are frustrated. How you’d like that...”

Fuck her
Decline

You check the clock on your codex for what seems like the twentieth time today as you approach the Romavian estate. Your heart suddenly lurches. What if she meant the time on Tenevo or something? But no, thank Void, here comes the princess herself, hurrying lightly down the driveway to haul open the gates.

“Steeeele. My clever [pc.boy],” she purrs, taking hold of both of your hands and leading you inwards. “It was foolish of one to even think you’d fail this test, wasn’t it? You hang upon every word I send you.”
Riya looms beyond her, looking enjoyably irked.
“[pc.He] had it way easier,” she complains. “I was supposed to notice your breakfast order was different? That’s bullshit.”

“You are the bodyguard to an heir of the Romavian throne, you overgrown ape!” Nastizia swishes a pale hand at Riya with magisterial contempt. “The slightest difference in my routine, the faintest suspicion of sabotage or subversion, should have you speeding to find the culprit. You have lost to darling Steele fair and square.”
She walks back up the steps. The expression she throws over her shoulder to you is sultry.
“Now. Let’s retire somewhere private and dish out reward and penalty, shall we?”
Next
You [pc.move] up to the security buzzer to the side of the gate, press it, announce that once again, [pc.name] Steele is here upon invitation.
As usual, Riya arrives to open the gate. But when you move to enter the estate you find yourself walking face-first into her
own face.
chest.
abs.
groin.
“Where do you think you’re going, champ?” she smirks, leaning against the gatepost. “This isn’t the time you were told to show up.” She nods at the pocket containing your codex. “Go ahead. Check.”
You grab your device and check the most recent invitation you were sent.
Another Cordial Invitation
From: nastinice@MegMess.org
To: [pc.name] Steele <@SteeleTech.corp>
To the honorable [pc.name] Steele esq.,

Hello darling! It’s me, your favorite princess. I got to wondering if you were still being a good [pc.boy] and reading these things properly, and decided a rum challenge would be finding out. So the time you have to arrive at my place tonight is seventeen hours. On the dot, you understand? Manage that, and you shall win the challenge automatically. I’m giving this one away to you dear, all I’m asking of you is an attention span. That’s how much I want you to win!
Yours faithfully,
1st Sc. Marvo Fauntilio

“I am most disappointed, Steele dear.” You can only stare beseechingly at Nastizia as she descends the front door steps beyond Riya, gazing at you glacially. “My invitations are not circulars, and the word of the heir apparent to the Romavian throne is not to be glibly assumed! You have lost this round, and failed to even amuse me in so doing.”
“Shall I turf this fool to the curb, kitten?” asks Riya, cracking her knuckles.

“Oh no.” The dazzlingly beautiful kaithrit gestures with a smirk, turning back towards the manor. “[pc.He] can still, and will, amuse me. Come come: I do believe it’s time to levy out prizes and forfeits.”
Next
You [pc.move] up to the security buzzer to the side of the gate, press it, announcing that once again, [pc.name] Steele is here upon invitation.

The response on the other side is silence, followed by a squawk of feedback. A minute later, a familiar brown-skinned human in a security uniform opens the front gate, jerks her head for you to come inside.
“Hands out to the side,” Riya says, before roughly patting you down. “Weapons in the gatehouse. You’ll get them back when you leave. I’m going to kick your ass this time.”
This last is delivered in the same cool tone, as if she were simply giving you directions. You smirk at her and carry on to the front door.

The response on the other side is silence, followed by a squawk of feedback. A minute later, a familiar brown-skinned human in a security uniform swaggers up to the front gate, lets you in with a little smile.

“Hands out to the side. Good [pc.boy].” Riya’s strong hands travel up and down your body, helping themselves to your [pc.ass], your [pc.fullChest], your neck and inner thighs. “Won’t lie: looking forward to having this as my side course tonight,” she murmurs in your ear. “You are, too. Don’t lie.”
She steps back and slaps your ass, directing you to the front door.
“You found the nerve to show up, did you?” The amusement in Riya’s tone carries clearly through the feedback. “Good.”
She strides out to the front gate and lets you through, patting you down in a way that might be construed as over-familiar.
“Weapons in the gatehouse,” she says, smiling widely at you. “I’ve been looking forward to this all day, Steele. Tonight, I’m putting you in your place.”
Your carry on without a word to the front door.
The enjoyably plump, maid-costumed rahn opens it for you. She blushes deeply when she sees you, and stumbles slightly to get out of your way.
The stern old kaithrit boy servant opens it for you.
“Darling Steele! I’m sooooo glad you could come.”

Nastizia is where she usually is, lounging in the ornately appointed living area in one of her many stunning and tastefully-revealing outfits. She grins at you coyly, taking a sip from her wine glass.
“Do go right through,” she says, waving at the facsimile villa in the dining room. “Remember dear, no over-indulging. On food, anyway.”

Grilled, oily, eel-like fish; citrus fruit and udon-like noodle stuffed into crisp vine leaves; wine so dry and white it looks like sparkling water; plump Tenevan ishis and jerris baked in brown sugar that you dangle into your mouth by their tails; smoky Roshan tea to follow.
You so rarely get high quality kaithrit home cooking on the frontier, and you find it difficult to control yourself in front of it.
You are getting a taste for this stuff. It must be even better on Tenevo itself, where the ingredients are fresh.
The white-furred kaithrit picks at her food and bats her eyelashes, whilst Riya leans on the door, eyes fixed on the fake middle distance. Both are eager for you to finish.

original: Back
You’ve gathered that she needs to prove her fecundity in order to have a solid claim to the Romavian throne. What is she planning on doing, once she is pregnant?

“Oh Steele darling, you’re here to entertain me!” Nastizia slaps lightly at your arm in admonishment. “I refuse to bother myself with serious details like that now. I am here to live in the moment - we are on the frontier, for goodness sake! But - if you must know...” she sighs deeply. “One supposes that she will have to present herself back at home. There will be much to discuss with the family. One’s siblings and cousins might attempt... hanky-panky.”
Hanky-panky? How many siblings does she have?

“Four. No, wait - three. Poor Hortenze.” Another deep sigh. “We may be mostly symbolic these days but there’s still a lot of power, and prestige, and money on the line here, dear. Most of my siblings are grown-up and I get along with them just fine (except for Miklias of course, what a stiff), but some of my cousins are very ambitious, and the legalities of it all are... vague. Centuries old. One must be prepared to draw her claws, even if one’s claim is paramount. That has always been the Tenevan way.”
Another light, brisk slap.
“I don’t wish to talk about it, Steele. I wish to have as much fun as I can, with the time I have remaining. Have you finished with your food quite yet?”
What next?

original: Riya is very tall for a human woman, standing 6' 2\" by ancient Imperial measurements. She has midnight-black hair in a crew cut, smooth, dusky skin and deep, intense brown eyes. She is in remarkable shape, her rolled-up sleeves showing forearms corded with muscles that flex powerfully with every slight movement she makes. She has two heavy, round, firm D-cup breasts, a flat, hard stomach, and slender, powerful thighs that propel her forward with panther-like grace. She has one tattoo, a lone ebony wing on the left side of her neck just under her jaw.

She is clad in a U.G.C. Peacekeeper uniform and dark blue beret. As you gaze on her form, you notice a rather conspicuous bulge down the left leg of her pants... it seems officer Batra is packing a little something extra.

original: You push your plate away, offering your compliments to the chef.
“You should save those,” husks Nastizia, sapphire eyes heavy-lidded, “until you’ve had the real main course of this evening, darling.”

She stands and sashays out of the holographic dining room into a darkened side chamber, small delectable rump swelling and scrunching against its expensive covering. You glance at Riya, and see she’s looking at the same thing.
“I don’t know what she has planned, if that’s what you’re wondering,” she says, giving you a brief, hard smile. “I didn’t cheat. I don’t need to.”
“Still haven’t looked,” she says evenly. “You’ve coasted on luck so far, Steele. Tonight, things change.”
“Don’t know why you’re looking there. That isn’t what you’re getting.” She smiles at you wolfishly. “I know what you really want, Steele. And I’m going to deliver it.”
“It’s good of you to put up a fight,” she says with a toothy smile. “It’s going to make me winning and knocking up your cute girlfriend there all the more gratifying.”
Next

Nastizia approaches you, her kittenishness replaced by a strangely intense vulnerability, brought out perhaps by her pale, flawless skin. Her eyes are pools of blue; the scales on her neck shimmer when they catch the light.
“You may kiss one,” she murmurs. “If you wish.”

You have macheted your way through sprawling jungles of steaming sexuality since you came to the frontier; every one of your senses has been subjected to sustained erotic bombardment, giving them the hardened refinement of a being centuries older than your real age.

And yet in this moment you feel rendered a fumbling adolescent. You must make conscious effort to prevent your [pc.hand] from trembling as it slides around the kaithrit’s slim, firm waist, drawing her close enough to feast upon the scent of that exotic neck and pale chest; the crinkle of her dazzling outfit beneath your [pc.skinFurScalesNoun], the touch of her hip and tummy, makes your breath catch slightly; and oh, the way she lifts her small, delicate chin to yours and then stops, drops her eyes demurely, drawing the moment out before your [pc.lips] do eventually press against her black tar, and you taste her breath.

It’s as chaste as a kiss on a prom dance floor at first; you part from her, gaze into dilated blue eyes and meet her lips with your lips again, and this time there’s hints of something darker, more exciting beneath the surface, beckoning you in deeper. Her cool fingers fasten themselves between yours and her hot mouth opens, enticing you inside; when you slip your [pc.tongue] beyond her pointed teeth she whimpers, so quietly that you’d think you’d imagined it if you hadn’t tasted the vibrations. Is her mouth particularly sensitive? Is her neck indicative that she’s modded...?

She pulls away like a silk scarf brushing down your arm before the thought can fully form, gently biting and pulling at your lip as she does. It’s only when the kaithrit is standing away from you, smiling smokily, that you realize how ragingly hard your [pc.cocksIsAre].

“I hope you enjoyed that, [pc.name] darling,” Nastizia says softly. She raises her voice, eyes full of feline malevolence as she turns them to Riya. “And I do hope it moves you to even greater heights of cunning and bravery in the jousts ahead. "+({var0}?"You’ve already seen what might be yours, all yours if you can turn this contest around...":"Why, just imagine what one might be willing to bestow if you impressed me again...")+"”

original: Riya is poker-faced; but her brown eyes are livid, fixed upon Nastizia, and her fatigues do nothing to disguise the fact that she’s nursing a hard-on at least as acute as yours. Seeing you get one over her in such an elemental way was tough for the big human, and when she forces her gaze over to your own big dopey grin she doesn’t need to say anything for you to know she’ll do everything in her power to prevent it happening again.
The chase, yes, and this feeling of intense rivalry; these are elements which are usually missing from the plentiful amounts of action you see elsewhere.

“Another invitation will soon be in the mail dear,” Nastizia says as she leads you to the estate’s door. Her hand descends down your back, her claws extending to graze the small. “I do look forward to these evenings so. You will come back?”
Her gleaming, fanged smile remains with you long after the door has closed and you’ve gotten your gear back from the gatehouse.
Of course you will.

original: You watch Nastizia as she gazes at Riya with glittering, icy eyes, hands on her hips, an all-too familiar little smile playing over her black lips. She’s weighing up pulling the string a little further down the road, the unholy little minx.

“Darling Riya,” she coos. “My rock. My indomitable praetorian. You’ve done as you said you would and shoved the competition aside. Each time I thought I’d devised a challenge that would trip you up, you emerged the victor. A lady should not notice her security detail - instead, I profess, I cannot stop thinking about you.” A slender, snowy finger touches wet, black obsidian. “But we have been enjoying ourselves us three, haven’t we? It’s been a scrumptious past week. And dear Steele has been trying so hard. I wonder if it would be so bad to prolong the competition a while longer...?”
Riya is across to her in two quick strides. Nastizia gasps as she’s swept off her feet into burly, brown arms.

“I get it, kitten,” Riya says in an unusually tender tone. “You’re scared. You came up with a funny game so you could put off doing the thing you know you have to do, the thing you gotta if you want a shot at being someone real. Now it’s suddenly time to face up and get bred, and you’re nervous. Well, listen. I’m your bodyguard, and as long as that’s the case you’ll never get hurt. That’s true of you, and the kid I’m gonna give you. Alright?”
“O-ohh Riya,” coos Nasti, a gooey look on her face as she gazes up at the human, bodice gently fluttering.

“C’mon. I want to see what’s in that bedside cabinet of yours. You too, Steele.” She shoots a fleeting, fiery look at you that makes your heart quail (and, guiltily, warmth towards your crotch) as she turns with her glittering luggage to the grand stairs. “It’s time for second prize. Don’t feel bad - silver suits you a whole lot better.”
Once again it occurs to you that you could just turn and go. There’s nothing tying you to any of this anymore.

original: The room Nastizia leads you into is a long hall with a cream armchair at the end. Spotlighted, motionless, metallic figures line it, eleven on each side. One side is red, the other is blue.

“Simple, VI-operated military droids,” Nastizia says, lightly touching the jaw of one. With a whirr it lifts its square, featureless face as if to look at her. “The Romavian family used to use them as actual guards on nepeta barges, centuries ago.” She touches the SMG-like weapon in its hands next, and it presents it for inspection. “These give mild electric shocks to their targets, enough to debilitate unarmored organics and droids.”
On the armchair two of the electroguns are laid; Nastizia picks up each and with surprising alacrity flings them to you and Riya.
“You already know the drill, dears,” Nastizia says, turning to give you both a delicately-fanged smile. “The last time we did this went so excitingly - well, I just had to do it again!”
She sits herself down on the armchair, gestures at the SMGs laid on either arm. You each collect one.

“Each side is programmed to obey one of you,” the kaithrit says, sitting back in the armchair with a smug smile. “Riya: you will command Team Blue. My faithful bodyguard, it is your duty to protect one from being spirited away by kidnappers, who might do unspeakable things to one. You must arrest their leader!” She closes her eyes and wriggles slightly, revelling in the notion. “Steele, you will command Team Red. My brave, roguish outsider, you must successfully invade this house and seize me, spirit me to the outside in one piece.”
The armchair lifts from the ground. It floats directly upwards, to a hole in the high ceiling.
“I shall be in my boudoir,” the kaithrit princess calls down, wiggling the fingers of one hand at you. “Whosoever has droids still standing by the end, or completes the objective, wins.”
“Good luck. You’re going to need it,” Riya says, examining the weapon in her hand thoughtfully. “This game is my actual job. Kitten wants me to win.”
“This is where it turns,” Riya smirks, carefully examining the weapon in her hand. “I’m on home turf, doing my actual job. You may as well just let me put the cuffs on you now, Steele.”

“I thought kitten might go easy on you,” Riya sighs, examining the weapon in her hand. “But nah, she wants me to win just as much as you do. Let’s get this over with. Try and make it look convincing, ok?”

“Ooh, tough luck Steele,” Riya smirks, carefully examining the weapon in her hand. “I get to do my actual job and you have to pretend you know the first thing about taking a secure position. Why don’t you let me put the cuffs on you right now?”
Next

You order the red droids to follow you and they do so, clinking and clanking with you out of the front door of the Romavian temporary estate and around to one side. Sheltered in a grotto of garden crystals, one of your team of titanium terrorists helpfully beams a floorplan of the building onto the ground in front of you, highlighting Nastizia’s room on the second floor.

You could go for a frontal assault, force your way in with the whole team, rely upon your own skill in combat. Or you could try something cleverer. Split your droids up, lure Riya to one side of the estate, then come in and seize Nastizia from the other.
Brute Force

Auto Fail
Let Riya win.

You move around the generously sized res deck partition, considering the big square building from each side, before choosing two large, ground floor windows. You lie out of sight and wait half an hour - long enough, perhaps, for your opposite number to second guess herself - and then, with a terse flick of two fingers mimicked from the dozens of extranet serials you’ve consumed, you order your troops to breach.
“Moger moger.”

The vanguard smash through the panels of hard light, the fragments winking out of existence as they pass out of line of the generator. Almost immediately one of your droids is gunned down by the two sentries posted here, but the sheer weight of red bots jumping through the frames quickly overwhelm them. You step over their buzzing, twitching chassis and urge your team on, blitzing towards a servant’s flight of stairs that will take you to the second floor.

At the end of the long hall leading to Nastizia’s room another blue bot has been posted: sheltering behind an upturned table, it takes potshots at your team as they gain ground. As you crouch-walk into a side bedroom one of yours manages to crown the sentry and sends it tumbling squawking to the ground. You feel a stirring of unease. Why is this so easy?
In this spare room there’s a locked door; if you remember rightly this leads to an ensuite which adjoins Nastizia’s room. It looks barricaded. The front way in, meanwhile, is visibly off the latch.
The Front

“Ooh my!” cries Nastizia, excitement shining in her eyes. The kaithrit is huddled on the bed, dressed in a thin and frilly chemise. “My dastardly kidnappers! Come in and grab me, quick - there’s no-one in here, I forbade them...”

You order one of your charges to fireman’s lift her out; no sense in the best marksman on the team doing it. She squeals piercingly as her lovely bottom is hoisted up into the air, and your team hustle out of the room.

Two of your droids are blasted out of commission right in front of you. The one hauling Nastizia manages to turn before it too takes a shot to the torso and goes tumbling down. original: “She’s really gotten into it, hasn’t she? What larks!” whispers Nastizia, hands over mouth.

You ignore your opponent’s taunts and consider the situation with a cold sniper’s eye. You’ve stumbled into an arena as well suited to one against nine as the one could reasonably hope for: there are many arched doorways out of the atrium, but all of them can be seen reasonably well from the furniture below, and none of them offer any cover. What will Riya do? Sacrifice droids to draw you out for a clear shot. That’s what you’d do. You will have to try and bleed her, frustrate her.

You put one finger to your lips and point at the floor with another. Just about containing her giggles, Nastizia lies flat as a pancake behind the lounger. You sit and wait, eyes trained on the archways above. Minutes crawl by. The click of drumming claws intrudes.
“One is getting bo -”

Four droids burst in simultaneously from different directions, their fire cracking and zapping off the floor and furniture, little arcs of electricity crawling off them. You remain perfectly still for a few more seconds, carefully note each’s path, then rise and fire four precise, circuit-halting shots, immediately flatten yourself. A white bolt whips past your [pc.hairNoun] close enough to
make your fur raise
to make your [pc.skin] cringe
make your hairs raise

Where did that come from? A flash of brown from the upper right hand door, you’re sure of it. A better angle... you put your butt on the tiled floor and with a thrust of your [pc.legOrLegs] slide across to the cover of the leather chesterfield. Not a second later four more droids pile in through the lower entrance opposite and make the lounger shudder and crackle with fire. Nastizia buries her head under her arms and screams fit to burst. You aren’t paying attention to anything but that upper right hand door... nothing exists but that upper right hand door... there’s nothing in the universe but that splash of brown in the upper right hand door... you raise your gun and fire.

Riya gasps and spits an inventive ethnic slur as she drops her gun, current shuddering through her. Swiftly you get to work on the remaining droids. Without their commander, they’re little more than roombas with laser pointers attached. In a few moments, you’re standing alone in the centre of the atrium, weapon rested on your shoulder, the jerking, crackling corpses of team blue arrayed all around. Warm hands and arms wrap themselves around your [pc.legNoun].

“What a performance!” exclaims Nastizia, blue eyes shining up at you with delight. “What drama! The way you got your whole team destroyed just so you could put this showdown on for me, [pc.name]... oh, you are a winner’s winner, there can be no doubt!”

Yes. Yes, that’s definitely what you were planning all along. You smile down at her with the roguish twinkle you’ve perfected, and turn the same expression on Riya when she emerges scowling on the landing above.
“You got owned,” she gristles. “One lucky shot right at the end, that’s all you had.”

“Oh don’t be such a sore loser, darling,” Nastizia smirks. Her hand slides up your [pc.thigh] as she gets to her feet. “Come come - let us three retire somewhere private and administer prizes. And, ah, forfeits.”
You grit your teeth and tighten your grip on the electro smg, resolving to make this as difficult for her as you possibly can.

And you do pretty well, in the circumstances. When droids begin pouring in from every arched doorway, you take down one, then another, then are quick enough to duck back into cover before the white, crackling barrage aimed your way finds you. But they’re in the room now, circling around you, and it’s only a matter of time. When you emerge again to try and pick off the one crawling down your three o clock, Riya nails you right in the "+({var0}.hasBreasts()?"tits":"chest")+" from her position on the landing above. You cry out and convulse, reactively hurling your smg away from you. When you recover, it’s to find the ripped form of Riya standing over you, smirking triumphantly.

“You’re so utterly without mercy, darling.” The kaithrit hisses the word with delight, stroking the human’s muscular arms. “What a ruthless display you put on!” She turns her half-lidded sapphire eyes on you. “Come along, dear. It’s time to administer prizes! And it would hardly be a prize if there wasn’t a forfeit to go with it, would it?”

You take a couple of steps back then charge at the door, slamming into it shoulder first. It parts from its hinges cleanly, snapping the table that had been leaned against the other side in half. You stampede through the ensuite with a bestial roar, making the kaithrit waiting in the bedroom beyond jump with fright.
“Oh my Steele darling, you gave one quite the shock! What an alarming kidnapper you make! Quick quick - there’s no-one in here, I forbade them - ”

You order one of your droids to firelift her out; no sense in the best marksman on the team doing it. The droid does so, lifting a squealing Nastizia onto its metal shoulder. Avoiding the main door entirely, you swiftly marshall your force out the way you came.

It’s at the head of the stairs you encounter problems. You didn’t go where Riya wanted you to, but she’s adapted swiftly, moving her forces around. Blue droids burst out of nearby rooms and open fire; below, the big busty human bursts into view, running and gunning as she barks at the droids with her to take positions behind the drawing room furniture. She takes out red droids with remorseless precision. The one carrying Nastizia gets popped in the torso. Crackling and sputtering, it spills the objective wriggling and giggling hysterically onto the carpet.

You remain calm, ignoring Nastizia for the moment to crouch-sprint to the stairs, staying out of view directly behind one of your droids, eye and gun on the floor below. Riya darts out again, confidently nailing the droid in front of you before it can fire... gifting you a clear and easy shot on the human herself. You land it on her clavicle with a satisfyingly meaty ZAP, stopping the human in her tracks and spilling her brawny bod onto the floor.

Riya’s fluent curses in your ears, you marshall your team to form up and take out the remaining blue droids around you. Without their commander they know little more than how to take cover and fire, and soon enough you’ve brought them all down. You stand amongst the crackling, zapping, gently smoking devastation with your three surviving bots, smg rested on your shoulder. Warm hands and arms wrap themselves around your [pc.legs].

“What a performance!” exclaims Nastizia, blue eyes shining up at you with delight. “What brutish, bestial will brought to bear! Ooh, I’m coming over faint just thinking about you bursting down that door... you are an animal, Steele darling!”
You smile down at her with that roguish twinkle you’ve perfected, and turn the same expression on Riya, scowlingly brushing herself down below.
“I’ll admit,” she says. “I wasn’t expecting you to cause actual property damage just to win a dumb laser tag contest.”
“Oh don’t be such a sore loser, darling,” Nastizia smirks. Her hand slides up your [pc.thigh] as she gets to her feet. “Come - it’s time to administer prizes. And, ah, forfeits.”

You’re torn between hurrying across to grab Nastizia and watching the floor below for a moment... and Riya uses that moment to rise and nail you right in the chest with her zap gun. You yell, fall struggling and convulsing with the charge, and lose your own gun in the process. By the time you’ve managed to stagger to your feet the rest of your team has been felled, and you can only watch as a smirking Riya strides up the stairs and scoops Nastizia into her arms.

“What did I tell you, kitten?” She reaches down and hauls Nastizia into her arms. “You’re in safe hands as long as you’re employing me. At least if every kidnapper is as much a meathead as this "+{var0}.mf("idiot","bimbo")+".”

You clock one cold as you like, then dart into an adjoining room for cover, white energy bolts crackling and melting the varnish off the nearby woodwork. Your friendlies draw the remaining two out to try again, and this time you are able to lead your fire and take both of them out. You step over their twitching chassis into Nastizia’s room.

“Ooh my!” cries Nastizia, excitement shining in her eyes. The kaithrit is huddled on the bed, dressed in a thin and frilly chemise. “My dastardly kidnappers! Come in and grab me, quick - there’s no-one in here, I forbade them...”
You do the honors yourself, hauling her light, soft weight onto your shoulder. The kaithrit comes with a delighted squeal which surely everyone in the house can hear.
Sure enough, as you’re emerging back into the hallway, you hear the thunder of approaching feet from the stairway at the opposite end you came in. Time to be ruthless.
“You two, there,” you command, nodding at the table barricade. “Hold ‘em for as long as you can.”

“Did you really sacrifice your whole team just to get your paws all over me, Steele dear?” coos a musical voice just beyond your ear. “That’s so scrumptiously ruthless of you! That’s so... oop!”

You’ve sat her bottom down on the bathroom’s windowsill. Managing to summon a gentleman thief’s voice from somewhere, you tell the good lady that she must climb down... or else you don’t know what you’ll do. Eyes shining, she hurries down the ladder, and a moment later you follow - just as Riya bursts into the bathroom, SMG in hand. You haul outside air into your lungs. Tastes like victory.

“That was wonderful darling,” Nastizia is saying, tails wrapping themselves around your [pc.feet] as you stroll with her back through the main entrance. “So devilish of you! Oh, it would make a marvellous plot for an extranet drama. I know some chaps on Nueva Monica you know, we should look into it... ”

“You lost your whole team, and in real life I would’ve capped you from the window,” scowls Riya, emerging in the atrium, throwing her zap gun onto a chair. “But sure. Technically you won. Drink it in.”
“Oh Riya dear, don’t be such a sore loser,” Nastizia titters, waving a hand at her. “Come - it’s time to administer prizes. And, ah, forfeits.”
Next

You level your gun, aim at the nearest droid, fire, miss... and in response it plants a stun bolt right in your neck. You cry out in pain, staggering backwards as electricity judders through you, losing your gun as you do.

You manage to recover, clamber back to your feet, grab a discarded SMG... but Riya has heard you. Before you can take care of the sentries around Nastizia’s door, a thunder of feet announces her and the rest of her team emerging at the opposite end of the hallway. A blizzard of bolts puts your two remaining bots and you back on your ass again. You are powerless as a smirking Riya strides over.

“That little decoy of yours was quite clever, I admit.” She reaches down and hauls Nastizia into her arms. “Shame you couldn’t plan for the fact you’re too much of a "+{var0}.mf("sissy","bimbo")+" to overcome a bot.”

You line all of your droids up outside the front in plain sight, then lead them in a Light Brigade charge straight through the main entrance. In a spectacular if brief light show, you and your whole team are sat on your ass by the killzone Riya has constructed around the foyer with her own droids.
“Oh dear,” you smile, clambering back to your [pc.feet]. “It looks like Riya has won!”

“Damn right,” the statuesque human smirks, twirling her shock carbine. Her eyes flash at you; she probably knows you just threw the competition. You don’t think it will change how she treats you one bit.

“Gosh, that was quick!” Nastizia picks her way through the gently smoking debris. “Very well then - I suppose it’s time we retired somewhere quiet to discuss prizes.” She strokes both your arms. “And forfeits.”

That last blow sends Riya staggering backwards, tears in her eyes from the pain of receiving yet another electroshock. You raise your plasma foil again, and she recoils.
“No! No. You - you win,” she mutters. SPPPHHHT goes her blue blade as she switches it off.

Riya is drenched in sweat now, wheezing for breath; you’ve run the athletic woman ragged. It’s the easiest thing in the galaxy to feint one way, and when she throws a tired block that way to come in from the other and tap her sword-hand with your blade. Her blue plasma foil drops crackling and sparking to the floor.
“Fuck,” she gasps, clutching her hand. “Alright f- fine. You win.”

“Oh, that was splendid! One feels like one has stepped backward in time!” Above you, your spectator is pealing her delight at the roof, clapping the rests of her hoverchair excitedly. “My two paramours, sweaty and passionate, ramming and rutting against each other, all to win one’s hand... it’s so romantic! You have both pleased me greatly.”
The chair descends to its former spot. Nastizia sighs as she gets up and sashays down the hallway, extending her hand.

“However, in contests of romance: there can be only one winner. Steele, darling.” You take her cool, white hand in your own roughened, hot sword hand, gaze into her glittering smile. “It is time for your reward.”
Next

You are brought to your [pc.knees] by that last blow, your entire body feeling like it’s glowing with electric pain. A harsh blue bar of light is brought to your neck; you grimace up at Riya, and click your own weapon off. You’ve lost.

You are completely exhausted now, soaked in sweat and almost doubled over with stitch, barely even able to hold your blade up. Riya moves about you with athleticism you can’t match; she has outlasted you. She steps inward suddenly, disdaining the blades to grab you by the wrist, shoving the back of your head between her tits and putting you in a half-nelson. You can’t even begin to get out of it.
“Give in?” Your wrist is pulled a little further up your back.
“Yes!” you cry. “You win.”
Your hand is let go.

“Oh, that was splendid! One feels like one has stepped backward in time!” Above you, your spectator is pealing her delight at the roof, clapping the rests of her hoverchair excitedly. “My two paramours, sweaty and passionate, ramming and rutting against each other, all to win one’s hand... it’s so romantic! You have both pleased me greatly.”
The chair descends to its former spot. Nastizia sighs as she gets up and sashays down the hallway, extending her hand.
“However, in contests of romance: there can be only one winner. Riya, darling.” You can only watch as Riya takes her hand, gives it a little squeeze. “It is time for your reward.”
Next
The room Nastizia leads you into is a long marble hall with a cream armchair at the end.

No droids this time. Your brave boys must spend most of their time jumbled up in a storage closet somewhere. There is a small wooden table on either side of the armchair. Each one has a small metal tube lying upon it. Each one has a small metal tube lying upon it. Nastizia picks one up, her voice echoing along the large, bare room as she twists the device around in her delicate fingers.
“These are plasma foils, antiques, from an age of chivalry long since past. In that time, the nobility of Tenevo dueled one another to settle matters of honor, blood-debt and love.”

The blade comes alive with a harsh, rasping PSSHHHT in her hands, a blunt, crackling, three-foot long beam of red light projected from the hilt. Nastizia’s face is flattened and stark as she gazes up at it.

“My great-great-great grandmother beheaded the Cate of Ongae in her own floating garden to claim the fair hand of Prince Lusch I, and the high throne of Tenevo itself. These were the very weapons they dueled with.” The blade cracks as it’s turned off. Nastizia turns a smile almost as blinding to you. “They’ve been modified with a stun field, of course. You two are not getting blood and limbs all over the floor. That would make one quite ill.”
“You want us to fight.” Riya picks the other one up, weighs it in her hand thoughtfully. “With these.”

“You will duel one another, gendarme. This will not be one of your grubby little wrestling matches with the station’s drunkards on a Friday night. These are matters of state and pride and desire that are being decided here!”

The kaithrit titters to herself as she flings herself back into the armchair. It lifts from the ground soundlessly on anti-grav projectors, and rises up about ten feet above the centre of the hallway. You pick up the plasma foil she left, [pc.move] across so that you’re below the aristocratic spectator, and face Riya. The foils are turned on almost in the same moment; the beam of lurid red light is quite weightless in your hand."+({var0}?" You are a skilled melee fighter, however you aren’t sure if any of the little tricks you’ve picked up through experience are going to be any use in this highly stylized format, wielding this thing instead of your trusty weapon.":"")

original: The blue of Riya’s blade touches yours lightly. The light hisses and crackles angrily. She smiles confidently as she steadies herself. You can’t imagine the big human has any proficiency with fencing, but she’ll certainly know how to use a stun baton.
“What a deeeelightful sight this is.” Nastizia sounds like she’s been at the nepeta. Well, she certainly is high right now. “When it touches the ground, my stout-hearted paramours...”

The white handkerchief floats delicately downwards. As absurd as all this is, your mouth is dry and your heart is pounding. You hold guard against Riya, not moving a muscle, not making a sound. It’s so quiet you actually hear the fabric when it drifts onto the marble. Both of you raise your blades and leap into action.

No droids this time. Your brave boys must be back in their storage closet. There is a small wooden table on either side of the armchair. Each one has a small metal tube lying upon it. Your heart beats a little faster.
Even though you won last time, these mano-o-mano duels are easily the most fraught competition the princess has thus far devised.
You remember the stress and pain of losing last time. These mano-o-mano duels are easily the most fraught competition the princess has thus far devised.

“I considered a number of other possibilities for games, but my mind just kept turning back to your last duel,” Nastizia says. She picks up the plasma foils and throws one to you each. “The artfulness, married to strength, and precision, and passion... oh, it is timeless, you see that don’t you? Why decide this contest any other way!”
“Good. I’m glad we’re doing this again.” Riya smiles at you challengingly. “No bullshit, just me versus you. This is what I wanted. This is where the comeback begins.”

“It’s not really sporting to hand a weakling’s ass to them,” Riya says, casually tossing one of the plasma foils and catching it like a baton. “But whatever. Let’s get this over with so we can get to the fun stuff.”
“Good. I’m glad we’re doing this again.” Riya smiles at you challengingly. “No bullshit, just me versus you. This is how it should be. This is how I win.”

Nasitzia is clapping her hands with delight as the armchair bears her up into the air, ten feet above the hall, so that she can get the perfect view of you battling her bodyguard with every inch you’ve got. You pace the hall with her; you turn your blades on with a harsh, crackling PSSHHHHT. They hiss as they meet in the middle. You hold guard against Riya stoutly, not moving a muscle, not making a sound; a white handkerchief drifts slowly downward. It’s so quiet you actually hear the fabric when it drifts onto the marble. Both of you raise your blades and leap into action.

original: Beneath your very hands she disintegrates, a swarm of purple insects that fly between your fingers and then blink out of existence. Within seconds all that’s left on the plush leather beneath you is your sorry [pc.cockNoun 0] and a mini-drone attached to a tiny perfume dispenser. It hisses honeysuckle scent at you mockingly.
“Why, Steele darling! One didn’t hear you being announced.” You can’t help but jump guiltily as Nastizia sashays into the room behind you, grinning with dazzling evil. “Having fun, are we?”
“Look, the hard light projection came onto ME, alright?” you manage.
“You setting that up was sadism. You know that, right?” you say stonily.
“I - er - ” you stumble.

“I was told [pc.name] Steele would fall for the first pretty thing that batted her eyelashes at [pc.him], but I wasn’t expecting it to be quite that easy,” the white kaithrit says, shaking her head and tutting. “On one’s own lounger, darling? Really? Is this the regard you have for one’s invitations and delightful entertainments?” Riya appears behind her, smirking at you insolently. Nastizia lays a delicate hand on the human’s jaw. “Riya dear wins this round.”
From the deep hole you’re currently inhabiting you don’t particularly want to talk about Riya, but you suppose you should learn how she resisted a pheromone-soaked gel maid nonetheless.
“She insulted it severely, which I believe caused the VI to malfunction slightly,” Nastizia sighs. “So then she knew it wasn’t real. A crude way to win, but...”

“I knew she wasn’t real to begin with,” Riya retorts. “A rahn frog? C’mon. I just wanted to see how sophisticated it was. You need to invest in better tech, kitten.” She flicks a contemptuous look at you. “Better [pc.boyGirl]friends, too.”
“It’s prize awarding time,” Nastizia says softly. Her fingers descend, touching the top of Riya’s breasts. “Forfeits, as well.”

original: Before your eyes she disintegrates into a cascade of purple grains, falling down onto the couch and then blinking out of existence. Within a few seconds, all that’s lying there in front of you is a deactivated mini-drone attached to a tiny perfume dispenser.

“My word, Steele darling.” You turn to find Nastizia framed in the doorway, smiling dazzlingly. “Love has made you quite cruel, hasn’t it? To deny your own base urges and a poor lonely chit like that, all in vainglorious pursuit of me... I will admit...” She leans back against the arch, hooks a long, lithe leg back against the wall, lets a marvellously cruel laugh gambol around the chandelier. “...It does set one’s pulse racing. You win this round, dear.”
“I don’t see why.” Riya looms behind her, scowling. “I saw through it, too. It was obvious. A rahn frog? C’mon.”

“And that’s exactly why you lost, my frowny frumpy gendarme!” The white kaithrit lashes her tails at the bodyguard impatiently. “You did it so rudely and gracelessly. Steele, meanwhile... [pc.he] has the manners of a prince. Most suitable.” She smiles at you both, more sultry now. “Come along. It’s time for prizes... and forfeits.”

original: “When one came out to the frontier, one thought it wise to travel with a companion,” Nastizia says, stopping by a bulky sliding steel door. “One that could be completely trusted.”
“I had a little tickle last night... a tiny tickle,” Nastizia says, stopping by a bulky sliding steel door. “That perhaps Tzacha made the wrong choice last time? To err is to be Tenevan, after all.”

She clicks a button, and the door rolls back to reveal a dimly lit room and the earthy stink of the wild. Something within stirs; a yellow eye the size of a side plate catches the light outside. Smiling brightly, Nastizia nods at the door. You wait until Riya shuffles inside before following suit.

The "+({var0}?"creature squatted in the corner of":"forklift-sized creature sat in")+" the large, tiled room you enter looks like a cross between a tiger and a toad. It is covered in lustrous, reddish-yellow fur and otherwise has all the physical characteristics of a large cat - the tawny eyes, the pointed ears, the short snout, the padded, hook-clawed paws. But it has the squat, low build of a frog, its back legs splayed, and its mouth is huge, face far wider than it is tall, lined with tiny fish hook-like fangs. It has two tails, kaithrit-like, that end in bulging, purple, pustule-like clubs. Its tawny eyes bulge from their sockets as it turns ponderously around to consider you.

original: It’s also somewhat larger than a tiger.

“I have looked after Tzacha ever since he spawned,” Nasti says, skipping across to the thing and squeezing him tightly around the neck. Tzacha emits a noise like a submerged car horn and rubs his cheek back against her. “And he has looked after me. I trust his judgment of people innately, do you understand? Innately.”
She turns her icy smile on the pair of you.
“And so, your task, darlings: Earn his trust. Feed him by your own hand. The one that performs the best, they shall win the tie.”
Tzacha grins at you. It’s what the Cheshire Cat would look like in an acid nightmare.

“Well, my oldest friend shall be getting another chance at appraisal,” Nasti says, dancing across to the thing and squeezing him tightly around the neck. Tzacha emits a noise like a submerged car horn and rubs his cheek back against her. “It seems only fair. Whoever wins will be seeing a lot more of him, after all!”
She turns her icy smile on the pair of you.
“You know what you must do, darlings: Earn his trust. Feed him by your own hand. The one that performs the best, they shall win the tie.”
Tzacha grins at you. He remembers you.
Next
What will you try?
Riya goes first. From the trough on the wall she hefts a big, dripping chunk of red meat and then slowly approaches the furry, fanged behemoth.
“C’mon Tzacha,” she says thickly. “You know me. You’ve seen me, protecting your mistress. We’re friends, aren’t we?”

Tzacha growls low in his throat, crouched on all fours in one corner. He makes to leap as she draws close, and Riya twitches; however, she manages to hold her nerve, and extends the meat slab. A long purple tongue snatches it out of her hands; it’s gone in a spatter of saliva and a ravenous gulp. Tzacha turns away from Riya, interest lost.

“Well doooone, gendarme!” Nasti’s tone drips with condescension. “Oh, I know you aren’t at all an animal person so that was very impressive indeed! Alright [pc.name], now you. He’ll be less hungry now, so it’ll be easier. Or harder, because he’ll want to play instead. We’ll see!”

Surrender immediately.
You [pc.move] into the center of the room, put your hands on your hips, and stare at the creature right in the bulbous yellow eyes.
“Hey. Hey you,” you say, loudly and clearly. “You want to eat?”

Tzacha stares at you. His teeth glitter in the dim light and he shifts, crouching lower into a predatory stance; his tails flick back and forth, clubbing against the wall. A growl like an approaching truck sounds deep in his throat. You consider this display with the contempt it deserves.
“You want to eat?” you repeat. “Then show the knee.”
Tzacha continues to lock eyes with you... and then his growl tails off, and he looks away. You ignore the murmuring of amazement behind you. It’s important to drive this through.

“That’s what I thought,” you say, [pc.moving] across to the meat locker and grasping another hunk of meat. The big soft galoot actually lets you feed it to him by hand; a hundred fangs each the size of a needle carry it into the wet, foul-smelling abyss. You reach up to scratch him behind his ear, and he growls and snuffles with delight.

“Steele darling, that is quite incredible!” cries Nastizia. She joins you, patting the monster on the nose fondly. “My maid almost lost her arm trying that. But oh look at you, it’s as if you’ve known Tzacha all your life. Isn’t he sweet once you get to know him?”
You depart the holding pen with a scowling Riya in tow. The smell lingers somewhat.

“Well, I know now exactly who my dearest companion is pointing me towards,” Nastizia smirks at you two, back in the foyer. “And they have won this round. I believe it’s time for prizes... and forfeits.”

“Hey. Hey you,” you say, loudly and clearly. “You want to eat?”

Tzacha stares at you. His teeth glitter in the dim light and he shifts, crouching lower into a predatory stance; his tails flick back and forth, clubbing against the wall. A growl like an approaching truck sounds deep in his throat. Maybe... maybe the bold approach wasn’t the correct one.
“Look,” you say haltingly. “If you want to eat, you better just - ”
Tzacha roars and turns to whip his tails at you. You feel the air move past your brow as you jump back. That would have easily crushed your skull.

“Enough! That’s enough.” Nastizia darts out and grasps Tzacha by the throat again. He calms down, but he continues to stare at you angrily. His yellow eyes are joined by Nasti’s reproachful blue ones. “Oh Steele, you really shouldn’t do that, not on his home turf! That was most ill-judged of you.”
“It was funny at least.” Riya puts on a high-pitched and entirely unfair impersonation of you. “Oh you better obey me Mister Tzacha, or I might just piss my pants at you!”
You depart the holding pen. The way the smell lingers doesn’t improve your mood.

“Well, I know now exactly who my dearest companion is pointing me towards,” Nastizia smirks at you two, back in the foyer. “And they have won this round. I believe it’s time for prizes... and forfeits.”
It seems as if the way to Tzacha’s heart is through his stomach. Maybe next time you could come prepared with something he likes to eat. What might a big cat-frog from Rosha like to nibble on?

You remain still and relaxed as the massive frog-cat-thing pads slowly towards you, takes big gusts of your smell into his wet nose. You don’t flinch when his wet, rough tongue slathers up your arm. When you put your hand out to stroke him behind the ear, he doesn’t move away; he simply gazes at you expectantly as you scritch him. Finally, you go fetch another hunk of meat and feed it to him by hand. Another smelly wet lick is your reward.
“Void,” Riya sneers. “If that’s what winning looks like, I’d sooner lose.”

“I’m glad you feel that way Riya dear,” Nastizia replies coolly, “because Steele has clearly triumphed. Look at how well [pc.he]’s getting on with Tzacha, after only a couple of minutes with him! Oh, that is most impressive darling. Isn’t he sweet once you get to know him?”

“Hey there, champ!”

You put your arms behind your back and do your best to smile at the beast sincerely. It’s difficult when he’s swishing his tails back and forth, batting at the walls, gazing at you hungrily. A growl like an approaching truck sounds in his throat.
“We can be friends, right?” You radiate harmlessness as best you can. “I’ll fetch you a chunk of meat if you’ll be my friend.”
He approaches you slowly, gives you a long appraising sniff. You try and relax. It’s working, this tactic is working...
Then he eats you.
“Tzacha! Drop it. Drop it. You look at mama! Drop it this instant.”

Light shines into the slathering, foul-smelling darkness, and then in a great wet expectoration you find yourself disgorged onto the tiled floor. The first thing you see once you’ve clawed hot saliva out of your eyes and you’ve calmed down enough to even think is Riya leant against the wall, howling with laughter.

“Ah yes, you ah - need to be slightly more imposing than that I’m afraid Steele,” says Nastizia, swiftly leading a snuffling, muttering Tzacha back towards his bed. “I’m very sorry that happened. “How are things at the nursery?” you ask. Briget tuts at you, saying that you’re quite capable of looking at the holoscreen’s readouts yourself, but you simply say that you’d like to hear it from her. The head nurse doubtless can paint a more vivid picture than a few stale stat-displays.
“Oh, very well,” the gynoid teases, leaning back on her desk
in a way that makes those lovely big breasts of hers thrust out against her blouse.
in a way that makes her blouse look awfully tight.
<i>“At the moment, we’re caring for
"+{var0}+" of your darling children.
Quite the prolific breeder you’re becoming. Then again, what did I expect from a Steele?
your one and only child. You must be so proud!
all of no children. Do me a favor and get to breeding, dearest. Your father didn’t bring me out of retirement to sit here idle!
<i>“Our facilities are

somewhat upgraded thanks to you. Please, continue finding more staff and equipment out there on your adventures - everything you bring back makes the nursery that much better of a place for your children to grow up.
as your father bought them. We’re operating at near-perfect efficiency, and at budget.
<i>“The staff is

still more robotic than organic, but is becoming more diverse by the day. Thanks to your efforts, I’m sure we’ll soon boast the most colorful staff in the galaxy - a fitting way to operate, given what your father
correctly
expected of you and your brood.

We’re flush with everything we could ever need, all thanks to you, oh captain my captain. The way things are going, you might want to start asking some of your many, many half-siblings if they’d like to make use of the facility: we have more than enough staff.

largely robotic, with some specialists in education and infant-handling on retainer. The nurse-droid staff is quite capable, though, I assure you - and we both know I’m quite able to handle as many children as needed myself.
Briget flashes you a smile and taps on her Codex, glancing through the information at her display. “I believe that covers everything of note at present. Anything else, [pc.name]?”
“How’s the staff doing?” you ask. Things certainly seem a little more lively around here.

Briget smiles, directing your attention to the holoterminal on the wall. It flickers over to a status display, showing off current numbers of synthetic and organic employees working in the nursery, along with breakdowns of their roles and terms of employment.
<i>“Everything is going quite well, dear.

Though most of the nursery’s duties are still fulfilled by drones and nurse droids, having an organic touch has certainly made our little home away from home that much more vibrant and pleasant. Even I have to admit, children do seem to respond better to living caregivers... at least until they get to know me.” She chuckles pleasantly, though her eyes turn downcast rather quickly.

You’ve certainly found more employees than I would have expected. Our budget is a bit strained at present, but I believe the effects more than justify a bit of "+({var0}()?"dogecoin":"credit")+"-pinching here and there: I never expected the nursery to feel so vibrant and alive

! We’ve built a community here thanks to you, dear
. They say it takes a village, and I can certainly see the wisdom in that now. I simply know that your offspring will be the best and brightest the galaxy has to offer under our care
... when you have them, that is
Out of curiosity, you ask “If I find someone out there in the galaxy that would fit in well here at the nursery, can I offer them a job?”

Briget purses her lips a moment, thinking. “I suppose so. Technically speaking, I am in charge of all matters related to staff, but of course I trust your judgement, dear, especially in regards to your own children. If you find someone with skills or talents you think would make the nursery a better place for us to bring up your offspring, I would be happy to have them. There will need to be interviews and contracts, of course, but those should be a formality.”
That answers that, you suppose. Does she have any suggestions for staff she needs? Any particular skillsets you should be on the lookout for?

“Oh, I take care of everything we need here, strictly speaking,” Briget assures you. “But that doesn’t mean I always have the contacts to find us truly unique skills on demand. Your father had the foresight to make accommodations for a laboratory and bio-science center on the second floor, which could begin to provide all manner of benefits to your reproductive health - and your fertility/virility - were it staffed and equipped. We also have a modular living bay designed to accommodate your differently-abled children, and those who need non-standard conditions to grow. Finding specialist caretakers for these environments is within my purview, but finding additional experts who can better educate and care for Rush-space species may be easier for you than it is for me.”
So scientists and aliens, basically?

“Basically,” Briget nods. “Oh, and while we’re quite able to source milk and other foods from corporate sources, I’ve always found that children respond better to a mother’s touch... and nourishment. Of course I am equipped for such functions - I nursed you, after all - but I am still a synthetic, as is my milk.

original: You ping Briget over the nursery’s intercom and tell your old nurse that you’re thinking about taking an extended stay with her.
“Oh? That’s marvelous, [pc.name]. Say the word, and I’ll have your personal effects brought up from your ship.”

You affirm your decision with her and turn your focus to the bed, deciding that with so much free time suddenly on your hands, it’s time to catch up on some much-needed rest. It isn’t long before Briget arrives along with several of the nursery’s drones, carrying boxes of your personal effects: everything from spare clothes to your toothbrush, whatever you’ll need for your stay. Before she leaves you, the gynoid nurse gingerly approaches and places a hand on your [pc.belly].
“I’m very excited to see what the newest addition to the family will be, [pc.name],” she
says with the warm, matronly smile you’ve come to expect. “Now you just relax and let me and my staff take good care of you until the next Steele is ready to join us.”

You nod appreciatively, content to have someone experienced to care for you while you’re vulnerable - and for your offspring once you’ve delivered them. Briget chuckles at the complement, reaching in to give you a kiss on the brow before sashaying out of the room. You’re half surprised she didn’t try and tuck you in.

murmurs, guiding you back to the bed. Once the drones are gone she’s quick to straddle your [pc.hips], wrapping her arms around your neck and leaving her over-ample chest so close that you can’t help but nestle your head into the soft, pillowy embrace of her cleavage. “Just as important, I get to have you all to myself for a while. Now I want you to lay back and let momma take care of absolutely everything, my dear.”
Before you can respond, Briget’s hand reaches down,
wrapping around your [pc.cock]
slipping a pair of fingers across the lips of your quim
caressing your barren loins

. She smiles seductively, eyelids heavy and plump lips pursed; it’s only natural to follow her into a deep, long kiss, letting Briget lead you into bed and take care of your every need the way only an amorous nurse droid can...
Your first night in the nursery is more restful than you imagined it ever could be.

By the end of the second day, you can feel all the tension bleeding away: all the aches and the cares of your adventuring life seem so far away as you’re pampered by Briget and her staff. The matronly gynoid simply refuses to let you lift a finger for yourself, insisting that she knows exactly how to treat a pregnant Steele after all these years. Considering her breadth of experience, and how relaxed and contented you soon find yourself, it’s hard to argue the point.

original: Nyrean Huntress

You step up to the glass door separating your watery children’s living space from the drier climate of the nursery, peering through into total darkness. You give a glance to the nyrean huntress standing nearby, wrapped up in the Steele Tech uniform that shows off plenty of toned muscle and a bulge that looks like she’s packing a coconut in her crotch. Kid friendly, for sure.
<i>“Greetings,
captain
my "+{var0}.mf("king","queen")
” the huntress says, giving you a respectful incline of her head. “A pleasure to make your acquaintance.”
There’s clearly no need to introduce yourself, so you just return her gesture and ask what she’s doing here, exactly. What qualifies her to watch over your young princesses?

The huntress smiles demurely, folding her hands behind her back. “I’m afraid there’s nobody truly experienced with raising Water Queen anymore. My only qualification is that our queen tried to breed me several times. None of her eggs took, though not for lack of trying, or desire on my part. When you were impregnated, the Queen asked me to come and find you; to serve you as I would have her. I am ever her loyal servant, and it is my honor to watch over the princesses while they mature.”
That’s about as good as you’re likely to get, you suppose. For now, you ask how the princesses are doing.

“They’re well. Very well,” the huntress answers, turning towards the monitors on the wall. “They’re still sensitive to the light, and we’ve had to expand their living space - Queens of the Deep Water are solitary beings, and they like to keep their distance from each other. And from me, when they don’t wish to nurse or play-fight. You’ll be pleased to know they’re growing quickly, and growing strong, too. The Queen was worried her daughters would be anemic, if they survived at all; she’s quite happy to have been proven wrong.”
As are you. On that note, is it alright for you to go inside and visit?

“Of course! The princesses have been asking after you almost non-stop since their arrival. They’ll be overjoyed to see their "+{var0}.mf("father","mother")+" again. Head inside whenever you like - just be very loud when you do. They can be a little jumpy when they’re nesting.”

Most of your royal brood is quietly nesting, tucked away in the various half-submerged corners of the modified chamber and soaking underneath beds of fungus and leaves.")

original: As their caretaker suggested, you make sure that opening the door makes plenty of noise. The door itself is huge, befitting the size its occupants will grow to, and a long tunnel stretches out after it to help funnel out the nursery’s light so opening up doesn’t accidentally blind your subterranean spawn. You traverse the tunnel, stepping out onto a little sandy island at the water’s edge, not much more than three yards to a side.

It’s pitch black inside, save for the dim light filtering through the tunnel at your back. A pair of red lanterns have been set up at the water’s edge, hooked up to power cables on the bulkheads. You flip them on and close the door, leaving yourself in dim crimson glow. Remembering that the princesses are already able to read and write, and so presumably speak, you call out a “Hello?” into the shadows.
A long moment passes.

The water ripples in front of you, and a head of silky hair blossoms on the surface, followed by a pair of dark eyes. Unlike the Queen’s, your daughter’s are fundamentally human in shape and color, staring up at you with wild curiosity. She rises from the pool a moment later, revealing a humanoid upper half the same lustrous blue as her mother, wrapped up in a one-piece swimsuit from shoulders to her waist, where the sprawling, spidery crab-body begins in earnest.
“"+{var0}.mf("Daddy","Mommy")+", is that you?” the princess asks, suddenly smiling. “It is! Everyone, come look who’s here, quick!”
context: <> <> output("\n\n“"+pc.mf("Daddy","Mommy")+", is that you?” the princess asks, suddenly smiling. “It is! Everyone, come look who’s here, quick!”")

original: She scuttles up out of the water, and suddenly you’re made aware again of just how massive her species is. Once she’s on land, you’re left
staring more or less eye-to-eye with your young daughter. Her spindly legs put her on a much higher vantage than a human, and her more personable half is shapely and tall, built like a swimmer.
staring up at your towering daughter. She’s bigger than a leithan already, a bit awkward on her spindly land legs but riding so high that it’s hard to see her face in the gloom.

leaning back and staring wide-eyed at the titan you gave birth to. She’s enormous, easily as big as the Queen herself now, riding high on slender legs each taller than a human, elevating her so high that it’s hard to see her face in the red gloom. You can, however, see that the more personable half sitting atop the crab-like lower body has developed into quite the woman, voluptuous and strong and just as gorgeous as her mother.
If only the Queen could see her now...

Incubation Room

The incubation room is protected by a high-security door, making it accessible only to you, people you’ve cleared and select members of the staff. It boasts different chambers, each a carefully controlled environment sealed behind a plexiglass window, to make sure your eggs are hatching in the right temperature, pressure, humidity and other variables.

The eggs sired by Edan are currently incubating in one of those chambers. A digital display above the window indicates that there "+({var0}?"are ":"is ")+{var2}.numToText({var1})+" day"+({var3}?"s":"")+" left before they hatch. You touch the plexiglass and sigh, gazing lovingly at the speckled shells from which your half-leithan foals will soon emerge.

original: There’s a distinct lapine edge to this room now
, and it drowns out the presence of your other kids at times

. Many of your laquine children are playing with their food to the staff’s chagrin. Briget seems to be concluding her stern warning, nodding her head to you as she returns to her daily affairs. The leporine tykes, however, perk right back up when they see you in her place, waving and smiling as always.

Your laquine children have certainly organized themselves into the little lords and ladies of this room. Of the play that goes on here now, they try to be at the center of it. Hard not to be when there are so many boundless, determined rabbit-boys and rabbit-girls looking for any excuse to run, jump, and fall... then do it over again. When they see you, they all try to tackle you by the [pc.legOrLegs], eager to shower you with pent-up affection.

There seems to be a spur of the moment art club in effect. A bunch of your laquine children are arguing back and forth on the quality of three presented art pieces. Your opinion of the displayed canvases are that they’re typical of a child’s quality, but you appreciate the competitive edge two of your bunny-sons are showing. They’re even casting votes!
While most of your kids probably eat in the cafeteria, your laquine children seem to enjoy feasting at the far corner at their own big table.
Other children are welcome, but most have since had their meal.
Echoing squeals and giggles fill the air when they see you. So much positive energy is directed your way when they smile and wave at you.
You could snap a pic of this for their missing parent, show that sweaty Jumper what she’s missing out on.

Stepping into the caretaking area, you’re pleasantly surprised to find Roxy here, and she’s the center of attention to your kids
, mostly the laquines
. She’s focused completely on them, and doesn’t notice your entrance.

You seize the opportunity laid before you to quietly walk behind her and pinch her fluffy tail. Roxy seizes up, biting her tongue to kill a surging yelp. Your bun puts on her best death glare before turning around; her face softens when she identifies you as the perpetrator.
You curtly
You gently
tap the reactive rabbit’s shoulder and she whips around fearsomely, mouth open about to say something. Something aggressive most likely as Roxy sheepishly turns her head back to the children.
Your older kids certainly get a kick out of her reaction!
“[pc.name]. Of course it’s you,” she jitters cutely. “Came to visit the kids too?”
More or less. You’re just a bit disappointed she didn’t tell you she was coming!

“I was gonna but then I was too excited and caught up doing other stuff to remember. I was uh, sewing some toys for the kids. My teammates gave me a bit of sh-,” she catches herself, “-of hard time over it but I went through with it.”

Now that she’s told you what seems a little different here, you notice that all the children in the room have specially made toys in hand. They regard them as they would anything else: inquisitively, playfully. They really love to hug them, too! Wait, doesn’t that one look a bit like you..?

“They’re very popular with them!” Roxy smugly intones, only to have the look drop as one of your kids throws one of the plush toys at the back of her head. You try to hold back your laughter as the tricksy rabbit-
boy
girl
giggles, but then everyone else starts laughing, unstucking your meager restraints.
“Don’t encourage them!” Roxy chides, turning to face her diminutive assailant. “And you, "+{var0}+", shouldn’t throw things.”

original: While Roxy turns her attention back to the babies, one of the older tykes waddles over to you, handing over one of the homemade toys. Getting to look closer at it you find that
You pick up the thrown toy to inspect it and
they’re surprisingly well stitched for being shapeless masses with embroidered faces on them. They’re also soft to a ridiculous degree.
they seem a lot smaller than the ones you saw the first time. Much less of a choking hazard...
And... they also kind of look like you.
They’re looking even closer to what you’ve seen in the mirror!
Those [pc.ears] are unmistakable.
“What? Disappointed?” Roxy chuckles at you. “Only so much you can do with fabric, [pc.name]. I was a tailor before I became an athlete. Kinda need to know how to do that and do it well to get paid.”

You hum in response, watching one of your children try to stuff one of the toys into their mouth. You instinctively go grab at the toy - “Wait, I got that!” Roxy yelps; no, you do! Somehow you end up racing Roxy to the task and falling all over each other in an effort not to collapse on your
son
daughter
! The room lights up with laughter while you help each other back to rationality.
“That’s parenthood, right?” Roxy grins.

The two of you pass the time playing with your kids, a knot gently unraveling in your chest. Being here is a blessing and a privilege. It’s a miracle. You try to come up with more ways to feel good about it, and find yourself lost in a field of celandines. It’s funny, in a way. You’re supposed to be out there seeing the universe... but you don’t want to. You don’t want to leave this room, you don’t want to leave the giggles, the tiny little grasps at your hand, the gleaming smiles on your children’s faces...

“You know, I’ve got this covered. I’ll be here for a little longer. I’m just glad we caught up is all.” Roxy slaps your shoulder. “If you got places to be don’t waste time on my account, [pc.raceBoyGirlShort].”
Cheeks raised, you lean in for a smooch on her twitchy nose and stand. Until next time then.
“But one thing while I’m here, no sex. This is for the kids, got it?”
Got it.
“Just remember, this ain’t a booty call, [pc.name]. I’m here on downtime for the kids.”
Understood.

Shit. But you’re [pc.name] Steele. You can do this.
